page: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a low-memory virtual machine instance belonging to the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes 236 GB of local storage. The x86_64 CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, and single-threaded operations show top-tier performance (top 10%) in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed, with average read bandwidths but a weak write bandwidth of 23.53 GB/sec (bottom 25%).

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4lds_v7 offers a high-frequency CPU and dedicated compute resources paired with a constrained memory footprint, making it a resource-dense option for workloads that do not require large RAM allocations. It is suitable for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and workloads utilizing Intel AMX or AVX-512 instructions, while being less optimal for highly parallelized multi-threaded processing or memory-intensive applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dldsv7 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage. The instance exhibits strong single-core capabilities, scoring in the top 25% in stress-ng single-core benchmarks and the top 10% in single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance and large-block memory write bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads by avoiding the expense of unneeded RAM. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and tasks leveraging AVX-512 or Intel AMX instructions.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, 236 GB of local storage, and strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4lds_v7 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine with 8.0 GB RAM, 236 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for low-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
8.0 GB of system memory and 236 GB of local storage.
Strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression.
Support for AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic database operations.
